channel.title: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism.
channel.description: KillBait is a news aggregator developed by students and professionals in journalism and web development from Argentina, Spain, and the United Kingdom. Our focus is on providing readers with clear, concise, and relevant summaries of each news story.

item.title: What is KillBait?
item.description: Surely you've clicked on a news article with an eye-catching or sensational headline...

item.title: How KillBait works
item.description: Our editors, along with any registered user, can submit news they find interesting simply by providing the URL to the system.

item.title: Upcoming news
Six Men Arrested for Bidding on Construction Projects in Palisades Fire Zone Without Licenses
item.description: Six men have been arrested for allegedly bidding on construction projects in the fire-ravaged Palisades neighborhood of Pacific Palisades without proper contractor licenses, according to Los Angeles County prosecutors. The group, consisting of Luis Perez Silva, Antonio Herman Perez, Santiago Ismael Mejia Urena, Jecil Cruz-Herrera, Raymond Joshua Castorena, and Luis Mateo Perez Lopez, advertised their services online following the 2025 Palisades fire. Investigators from the California Contractors State License Board discovered their unlicensed bids totaling up to $1.27 million for construction work. Authorities allege the men operated without licenses and faced potential penalties including prison time and fines. Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman warned that hiring unlicensed contractors poses risks like fraud and unsafe construction. Five of the men face up to three years in prison, while Castorena, who has a prior strike offense, could receive up to six years. The ca

item.title: July 4th Market Closure Dates and Impact on Trading
item.description: The stock market will be closed on July 4th, 2026, for the Independence Day holiday, affecting trading schedules and investor strategies. This closure is part of the standard holiday observance in the United States, which typically results in reduced market activity. Financial institutions and trading platforms will follow the non-trading calendar, impacting short-term trading opportunities. Investors are advised to adjust their portfolios and monitor market trends around the closure period. The closure may also influence bond markets and other financial instruments, as liquidity can decrease during holidays. Historical data shows that market volatility often increases in the days leading up to and following such closures. Traders should be cautious about positions held near the holiday and consider liquidity risks. The Federal Reserve and other regulatory bodies may also release statements or data around this time, which could affect market movements. Overall, the July 4th closure ser

item.title: A Remote House in Iceland's Vestman Islands and Its Surprising History
item.description: The article discusses a house on Elliðaey Island, part of the Vestman archipelago in Iceland, often mistaken for the 'most solitary house on Earth.' Built by hunters in the 1950s to study and hunt fulmars, the structure is accessible only during specific weather conditions. Despite its remote location, the house has electricity and basic amenities, with a YouTube video showcasing its interior. The original viral claims about Björk or curses are debunked, revealing it was never her property. The article highlights the house's role in conservation efforts and its status as a unique travel destination, contrasting the sensationalized title with factual details. It also mentions a nearby island with a smaller population and a book compiling similar geographical stories. The piece emphasizes the blend of isolation and human activity in remote locations.

item.title: Fire Destroys Parking Lot Near Wilmington Motel, Authorities Investigate Cause
item.description: Authorities are investigating a fire that broke out Friday night at a parking lot near a Wilmington motel, where multiple vehicles were found engulfed in flames. The Los Angeles Fire Department (LAFD) reported the incident occurred around 8:30 p.m. at 1104 W. Pacific Coast Hwy. While the exact cause remains under investigation, officials suspect fireworks may have sparked the blaze. Aerial footage from CBS Los Angeles showed two charred cars and a blocked street. One male individual was hospitalized in critical condition. The Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) confirmed they are not providing further details at this time. This incident adds to a series of fireworks-related fires in Southern California, including a recent explosion in Pacoima that injured several residents. The article also highlights ongoing coverage of other local news, such as arrests linked to illegal fireworks and the demolition of a Boyle Heights warehouse. The LAFD spokesperson emphasized that while fireworks a

item.title: Scientists Create Synthetic Cell with Key Life Functions in Lab
item.description: Researchers at the University of Minnesota have developed a synthetic cell called SpudCell, which demonstrates fundamental life processes such as growth, DNA replication, division, and trait inheritance. The cell, constructed entirely from nonliving components, operates under controlled laboratory conditions requiring external nutrients and specialized components. While the study, published as a preprint on bioRxiv, represents a significant step toward artificial life, the synthetic cells cannot survive outside lab environments and rely on purified E. coli ribosomes. The team introduced genetic mutations enabling faster-growing cells to dominate populations, showcasing natural selection. However, the system remains less capable than natural cells, with only 30% of daughter cells inheriting the complete synthetic genome after five generations. The research highlights both scientific advancements and potential biosafety concerns, with future goals focusing on improving self-sufficiency a

item.title: Rockstar Games faces criticism over workplace practices and pay disparities
item.description: Rockstar Games, the developer behind the upcoming GTA 6, is facing allegations from union members regarding unfair workplace practices. The Rockstar Game Workers Union (RGWU) claims the studio enables crunch culture by embedding opt-out clauses in employee contracts, effectively normalizing excessive work hours. Union representatives also highlight a lack of transparency around pay and bonuses, with compensation packages often relying on fluctuating incentives that lack clear justification. Gender-based pay disparities have reportedly widened, and initiatives to address these inequities have been scrapped. Employees allege that promotions are difficult to secure due to inconsistent career progression criteria, creating a climate of uncertainty. The studio's compensation model, which heavily relies on discretionary bonuses, has led to concerns about job security and fair treatment. Take-Two Interactive, Rockstar's parent company, responded by emphasizing its commitment to employee welfa

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's Wedding at Madison Square Garden Draws Celebrities
item.description: Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce married in a lavish ceremony at Madison Square Garden, drawing hundreds of celebrities and sports personalities. The event, reported by OutKick Sports, featured high-profile guests such as Los Angeles Rams quarterback Matthew Stafford, Fox Sports commentators Erin Andrews and Charissa Thompson, and ESPN's Joe Buck. Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and his wife, along with Chiefs tight end George Kittle and former Chiefs teammate Kareem Hunt, were also in attendance. The couple opted for a minimalist approach, without traditional bridesmaids or groomsmen, instead having Taylor's brother Austin Swift as her Man of Honor and Jason Kelce as Travis's Best Man. The ceremony featured haute couture designs by Christian Dior and custom footwear from Christian Louboutin, with Cartier jewelry for the bride. While Chiefs head coach Andy Reid and quarterback Patrick Mahomes were expected but not spotted, the event highlighted the growing cultural intersection b

item.title: Fourth of July air show in Washington features historic military aircraft display
item.description: The article discusses the launch of a mobile application designed to help spectators track the Fourth of July aerial flyover in Washington, D.C., which will feature a nine-hour display of military aircraft including NASA jets, the Blue Angels, Thunderbirds, Air Force One, F-22 Raptors, and B-1 bombers. The app, developed by senior White House official Shawn Chittle, provides real-time flight data, adjustable countdown timers, and detailed information about each aircraft. The event, part of the America 250 celebrations marking 250 years of U.S. independence, includes scheduled flyovers starting at 1:14 p.m. with NASA F-5 jets and culminating in a nighttime B-1 bomber display at 10:36 p.m. The app aims to enhance the spectator experience by synchronizing with the air show schedule and offering historical context about the participating aircraft. The event highlights military aviation traditions and national pride, with the White House emphasizing its role in commemorating American herita

item.title: Oil Prices Stabilize Amid U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Uncertainty
item.description: Global oil prices have remained within a narrow range of $71-$73 per barrel for ICE Brent, reflecting market fatigue with geopolitical tensions and a lack of clarity on the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Traders are increasingly focusing on supply-side dynamics rather than political headlines, as the market anticipates potential disruptions from the ongoing conflict. Meanwhile, Iraq and Turkey are negotiating a temporary extension of the Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipeline agreement to avoid a complete shutdown by July 27. The European Union has expanded its operations against Russian shadow tankers, leading Cameroon to remove 39 vessels from its registry. Canada announced plans to build a new Pacific coast pipeline, reducing reliance on U.S. transits. Colombia's incoming administration is poised to overhaul Ecopetrol's management, signaling a pro-oil policy shift. Qatar has resumed LNG exports through the Strait of Hormuz, while Japan is shifting to cheaper coal due to LNG price volatility. The UAE'

item.title: Severe thunderstorm warning disrupts Trump's planned Mount Rushmore speech in South Dakota
item.description:
A severe weather system in South Dakota disrupted President Donald Trump’s scheduled appearance near Mount Rushmore, where he was expected to speak as part of a July 4 Independence Day celebration tied to the America-250 observance. As Trump traveled to the state aboard Air Force One, the National Weather Service issued a severe thunderstorm warning for the region, cautioning residents about hazardous conditions including quarter-sized hail capable of damaging vehicles and strong winds. Officials urged people to seek shelter in sturdy buildings and avoid windows as the storm approached.
The weather alert created uncertainty around the planned festivities and prompted real-time coverage changes by media outlets. Fox News, which was covering the event, briefly shifted its broadcast as anchors warned viewers of the incoming storm and advised those in the area to take immediate safety precautions. The situation raised concerns that outdoor portions of the Mount Rushmore celebration, inclu

item.title: MAGA activist promotes weather-control conspiracy theory involving liberals and Trump, drawing criticism
item.description:
A recent political controversy emerged after a MAGA-affiliated activist suggested that extreme weather conditions in Washington, D.C. may have been artificially manipulated. Kylie Jane Kremer, known for her involvement in organizing the Jan. 6 rally, posted on social media platform X claiming that individuals with strong anti-Trump sentiment had “geoengineered” the weather. She alleged that both unusually high temperatures during a current heat wave and historically cold conditions during Donald Trump’s inauguration were the result of deliberate manipulation rather than natural weather patterns.
Her statements quickly gained attention online, particularly after a meteorologist for The Washington Post noted that Washington, D.C. was experiencing temperatures hotter than 99 percent of the planet, with only the world’s most extreme desert regions reporting comparable heat. This scientific context contrasted sharply with Kremer’s claims, which lacked evidence and relied on broader conspir

item.title: Sony's Shift to Digital Gaming Sparks Concern Among Developers and Publishers
item.description: Sony's decision to discontinue physical disc production for new PlayStation games starting in 2028 has sparked widespread dismay among game companies and developers. The move, which will transition all PlayStation titles to digital-only distribution by January 2028, has raised concerns about the future of physical media in gaming. Companies like Iam8bit, Atari, and Silver Lining Interactive have expressed disappointment, emphasizing the importance of physical games for preservation, ownership, and collector communities. Sony's announcement also includes the closure of the PlayStation Store on PS3 and PS Vita by July 2027, eliminating future content purchases on these platforms. Developers argue that physical media remains vital for cultural preservation and consumer choice, with some companies planning to continue offering physical editions despite the industry shift. The Video Game History Foundation highlighted that while physical media is being phased out, most modern games were alr

item.title: Alibaba Banned Claude Code Due to Alleged Security Concerns
item.description: Alibaba has implemented a ban on employees using Anthropic's Claude Code in the workplace, citing alleged backdoor risks. This move comes amid escalating tensions between Alibaba and Anthropic, with the latter accusing Alibaba of illicitly extracting capabilities from its Claude AI model. The dispute highlights the broader competition between U.S. and Chinese tech giants in advancing artificial intelligence. Anthropic claims Alibaba engaged in a 'distillation' process, training a less capable model on Claude's outputs to accelerate China's access to advanced AI features. Alibaba's ban follows reports that Claude Code included mechanisms to inspect user environments, such as timezone and proxy data, and insert markers into prompts sent to Anthropic's servers. Anthropic attributes the feature to an experiment aimed at preventing account abuse by unauthorized resellers. However, enforcing restrictions on Chinese users remains challenging, as individuals can deploy servers in the U.S. to m

item.title: Colombian politician Abelardo Espriella symbolizes yellow shirt populism
item.description: The article discusses Abelardo Espriella, a prominent Colombian political figure known as 'El Tigre' (The Tiger), who has become a symbol of the yellow shirt movement. This movement, associated with populist politics, has gained traction in Colombia through its emphasis on grassroots mobilization and anti-establishment rhetoric. Espriella's leadership has been central to the campaign's visibility, leveraging his charismatic presence to attract supporters. The piece explores how the yellow shirt movement has evolved into a significant political force, challenging traditional power structures and advocating for economic reforms. It also highlights the controversies surrounding the movement, including accusations of corruption and the complexities of balancing populism with democratic governance. The article underscores the broader implications of such movements in Latin American politics, where they often serve as both a vehicle for social change and a source of political polarization. K

item.title: LGBT cruise faces restrictions as Turkey blocks port calls
item.description: A long-planned LGBT cruise organized by Virgin Voyages has encountered significant obstacles as Turkish authorities have canceled its planned stop in the country. The provincial government cited alignment with local 'structure and moral values' as the reason for the decision, effectively barring the ship from spending multiple days in Turkey during its voyage from Athens to Venice. This development has sparked discussions about travel restrictions and LGBTQ+ rights in the region. The cruise, which was anticipated to attract a diverse group of passengers, now faces uncertainty regarding its itinerary. Turkish officials have not responded to inquiries from The Washington Post, while Virgin Voyages has also been unable to provide immediate clarification. The incident highlights broader tensions between international travel and local cultural norms, particularly in countries with strict regulations on public displays of sexuality. Advocacy groups have criticized the decision as discriminat

item.title: Dispute Over Demolition Delays at Boyle Heights Warehouse Sparks Community Concern
item.description: Lineage, the company managing a fire-ravaged cold storage warehouse in Boyle Heights, accused Altus Power of delaying demolition efforts following a June 17 blaze suspected to have been sparked by the solar panel subcontractor. In a letter to Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass and Supervisor Hilda Solis, Lineage CEO Greg Lehmkuhl stated crews were prepared to begin removing debris but were ordered to halt work, potentially to preserve evidence. Altus Power denied obstructing efforts, emphasizing community safety and cooperation with investigators. The fire, which damaged 85 million pounds of rotting food, has raised health concerns among residents. Councilmember Ysabel Jurado urged all parties to prioritize cleanup over liability disputes, stressing the urgency of addressing air pollution and public health risks. Cleanup efforts face delays as officials balance evidence preservation with community needs, leaving questions about long-term environmental impacts.

item.title: Heatwave shuts down Trump's Great American State Fair as medical emergencies reported
item.description: The Great American State Fair hosted by Donald Trump on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., was forced to close temporarily due to record-breaking heatwave conditions. Reporter Julian Andreone described the event as a 'total disaster' with chaos unfolding as paramedics rushed to save attendees. Seven individuals were reported to be on 'advanced life support' in hospitals after suffering heat-related illnesses. The event, which had already been shut down earlier in the day, faced extreme temperatures exceeding 99% of global conditions, with Washington, D.C., being warmer than most of the planet. Attendees resorted to desperate measures, including one Trump supporter dunking herself in a baptismal pool to escape the heat. The fair's location in an open field with no shade exacerbated the situation, leading to a largely deserted scene as organizers planned to reopen at 5 p.m. Meteorologist Ben Noll noted the unprecedented heat, highlighting the severity of the conditions. The incident

item.title: Tornado Warning for Chatham-Kent – Rondeau Park in Ontario
item.description: Environment Canada has issued a tornado warning for Chatham-Kent – Rondeau Park in Ontario, indicating that a severe thunderstorm may be producing a tornado. The warning, which carries an extreme impact level and high forecast confidence, was issued at 7:28 p.m. on Friday. Residents are urged to take immediate shelter in basements, interior rooms, or sturdy shelters when severe weather approaches. If no shelter is available, they should lie in low-lying areas and protect their heads from flying debris. The storm could bring damaging winds, large hail, and locally intense rainfall. Additional safety measures include returning to shore if boating, moving away from the tornado’s path, wearing a lifejacket, and lying face down. The warning highlights the need for residents to monitor Environment Canada forecasts for updates and follow safety protocols to minimize risks. This incident underscores the importance of preparedness during severe weather events, particularly in regions prone to t

item.title: Federal Officials Declare Trump's July 4 Celebration 'Well-Prepared' Amid Heat Wave and Hospitalizations
item.description: Federal agencies described President Donald Trump's July 4 celebration at the Great American State Fair as 'safe' and 'well-prepared' despite extreme weather conditions. The event, part of the 250th anniversary celebrations of the United States, faced severe challenges due to a record-breaking heat wave that forced organizers to close the fairgrounds. The heat index reached 113°F, prompting authorities to implement cooling measures, expand water stations, and increase medical support. Public access was limited to 4 hours to reduce heat exposure, though Trump's scheduled remarks and fireworks display remained unchanged. Several attendees were hospitalized, and the event faced criticism for its empty grounds and inadequate preparation. The incident highlights tensions between public safety and large-scale political events, raising questions about the White House's responsibility for the Reflecting Pool disaster and the broader implications of extreme weather on public gatherings.

item.title: AI-Driven Attacks Pose New Cybersecurity Challenges
item.description: The article discusses the growing threat of AI-powered cyberattacks, emphasizing their ability to bypass traditional security measures and exploit human vulnerabilities. It highlights the need for robust cybersecurity strategies, including advanced threat detection, employee training, and multi-layered defenses. The piece also explores the ethical implications of AI in warfare and surveillance, warning of potential misuse by state and non-state actors. Experts stress the importance of international cooperation to address these challenges, as AI-driven attacks can target critical infrastructure, financial systems, and personal data. The article concludes with recommendations for individuals and organizations to adopt proactive measures, such as regular system updates, encryption, and incident response planning, to mitigate the risks posed by evolving cyber threats.

item.title: New England Patriots Coach Mike Vrabel and Wife Attend Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Wedding
item.description: The article discusses the public appearance of New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and his wife, Jen, at the wedding of NFL star Travis Kelce and pop singer Taylor Swift. Vrabel, who recently led the Patriots to a Super Bowl victory, was spotted in New York City arriving at and departing from a hotel with his wife, marking their first public appearance since the Dianna Russini scandal. The event drew significant attention due to the high-profile nature of the attendees, including other NFL legends and celebrities. The piece also touches on the controversy surrounding Russini, a former NFL reporter, who resigned from her position after photos of her with Vrabel surfaced, sparking public debate. The wedding, held at Madison Square Garden, is highlighted as a major social event with a star-studded guest list. The summary emphasizes the intersection of sports and celebrity culture, as well as the personal and professional implications for Vrabel following the scandal. The article u

item.title: Mother and Daughter Found Safe After Being Lost on Southern California Trail
item.description: A mother and daughter, Krystal Meyers, 42, and her daughter Alexis Meyers Martinez, 21, were reported missing after hiking on the Vivian Creek Trail in the San Gorgonio Wilderness area of the San Bernardino Mountains. Officials issued a missing persons alert on Friday, July 3, 2026, after the pair failed to return from their trek. The trail, known for its steep ascent to Mount San Gorgonio (11,503 feet), became a focal point for search efforts. The San Bernardino County Sheriff’s Department confirmed that the duo was found 'alive and well' by Friday afternoon, though no details about their ordeal were disclosed. The pair was uninjured and reportedly walked out on their own. Their last known location was at the 10,300-foot level above the High Creek switchbacks on Thursday. The incident highlights the dangers of hiking in remote, rugged terrain. Authorities emphasized that the hikers were not in distress and had no injuries, underscoring the importance of preparedness and awareness when

item.title: Analyzing Unemployment Metrics and Economic Health
item.description: The article critically examines the limitations of using the official unemployment rate as a measure of economic health. It highlights how the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) tracks multiple metrics, including U-3 (official rate) and U-6 (which includes marginally attached workers and part-time workers for economic reasons). The discussion revolves around debates over whether the current unemployment rate reflects genuine economic improvement or masks underlying issues such as underemployment, gig economy precarity, and disparities among demographic groups. For example, Generation Z faces stagnant wages and unstable gig work, while boomers grapple with healthcare costs and workplace devaluation. The piece also critiques how metrics like the Consumer Price Index (CPI) exclude subsidized costs, further complicating economic assessments. Additionally, it references the political dimension, noting how leaders like Trump have historically influenced labor statistics reporting. The article

item.title: Franz Laskoff: Pioneering Artist of the Belle Époque's Poster Art
item.description: Franz Laskoff (1869–1921) was a Hungarian-born artist and illustrator who became a leading figure in early 20th-century poster art and graphic design during the Belle Époque period. Working primarily in Italy and France, Laskoff is renowned for his elegant Art Nouveau style, characterized by graceful figures, flowing lines, vibrant colors, and decorative sophistication. His work included posters and advertisements for fashion houses, luxury products, theaters, and cultural events, often featuring ethereal, idealized women and a strong sense of harmony and ornamentation. Though his career was relatively short, Laskoff left a significant mark on the golden age of poster art. His creations, such as the 1895 Exposition Retrospective Alsacienne et Lorraine poster and the 1900 Cioccolatto Suchard advertisement, exemplify his ability to blend aesthetic beauty with commercial appeal. Laskoff's legacy endures through his contributions to the visual culture of the Belle Époque, securing his plac

item.title: Celebrity Wedding: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's Lavish Madison Square Garden Ceremony
item.description: The article details the highly anticipated wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce, which took place at the Madison Square Garden. Despite being reported as a private event, fans gathered outside the venue, showcasing the couple's popularity. The ceremony, described as lavish, involved meticulous planning, including privacy measures like nondisclosure agreements for guests. The event was compared to an 'American royal wedding' due to its scale and public interest. Key elements include the involvement of renowned wedding planner Mark Seed, the installation of a red carpet (later removed), and the emphasis on discretion. The article highlights the cultural impact of the celebrity union, blending entertainment and sports figures, and underscores the challenges of organizing such a high-profile event while maintaining privacy.

item.title: Nuclear Startups Reach Key Milestone in Reactor Development
item.description: Three nuclear startups have achieved a significant milestone by successfully activating new reactor designs as part of a Department of Energy pilot program aimed at advancing nuclear energy innovation. While this marks progress toward the so-called 'nuclear renaissance,' experts caution that commercialization remains far off. The companies, including Valar Atomics and Antares Nuclear, reached criticality—a key step in sustaining nuclear reactions—by July 4, 2026, under a Trump-era executive order. However, these prototypes are not yet viable commercial products; they are test reactors designed to demonstrate feasibility. Challenges include regulatory hurdles, high development costs, and supply chain complexities. Despite government efforts to streamline processes, such as reducing environmental reviews and fast-tracking licenses, experts like Adam Stein emphasize that the industry still faces significant obstacles. While investors and tech firms see potential in small modular reactors

item.title: MLB trade deadline 2025: Key team updates and player movements
item.description: The article provides an overview of the 2025 MLB trade deadline, highlighting significant transactions and strategic moves by various teams. It discusses how teams like the Miami Marlins are bolstering their roster with high-impact players, while the Houston Astros are targeting depth in pitching. The Tampa Bay Rays are making a bold move to acquire Tarik Skubal, aiming to secure a World Series berth. The Chicago White Sox are focusing on strengthening their pitching staff, and the Los Angeles Dodgers are pursuing top-tier talent to maintain their competitive edge. Meanwhile, the Toronto Blue Jays are seeking a starting pitcher to complement their offense, and the Baltimore Orioles are considering trading key players like Taylor Ward and Trevor Rogers to rebuild. The article also mentions the Miami Marlins' aggressive strategy to acquire star players and the Detroit Tigers' potential trade of Skubal to the Rays. These moves reflect teams' efforts to address weaknesses, capitalize on ma

item.title: Sea anemone study reveals alternative antiviral immune system and new evolutionary strategies
item.description:
Researchers have identified an unexpected antiviral defense mechanism in sea anemones that differs fundamentally from the immune strategies observed in humans and other vertebrates. The study focuses on a protein named CARDIB, discovered in sea anemones, which closely resembles the human antiviral protein MAVS but functions in the opposite way. While MAVS activates antiviral defenses in humans, CARDIB acts as a suppressor under normal conditions. Surprisingly, this suppression is not harmful; instead, it is essential for mounting an effective immune response against viral infections.
To investigate its role, scientists used CRISPR gene editing to remove the CARDIB gene from sea anemones. The results showed that animals lacking this protein became significantly more vulnerable to viral infections. Viral replication increased, immune responses were weakened, and overall resistance to disease dropped sharply. This counterintuitive finding suggested that the immune system of these ancient

item.title: Zohran Mamdani's Speech Contrasts MAGA's Vision with America's Inclusive History
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani delivered a speech commemorating the 250th anniversary of the United States, emphasizing the nation's inclusive history shaped by immigrants. He criticized MAGA's anti-immigrant rhetoric and policies, arguing that America's strength lies in its diversity and the contributions of marginalized communities. Mamdani highlighted how immigrants have historically built New York and other cities, contrasting this with Trump's exclusionary vision. He condemned the idea that America is an arena of supremacy for a select few, stressing that the nation's true essence is its capacity for progress and collective striving. The speech underscored the resilience of democratic ideals against authoritarianism, calling for a renewed commitment to equality and social justice. Mamdani concluded by celebrating the everyday struggles and hopes of ordinary Americans, framing the nation's journey as one of continuous improvement.

item.title: Fox News host's outburst highlights escalating tensions in tight Texas Senate race
item.description: Fox News host Emily Compagno delivered a fiery on-air tirade against Democratic Senate candidate James Talarico, calling him a 'demon in human skin' during a discussion on her show 'Outnumbered.' The outburst came as the Texas Senate race tightened into a genuine toss-up, with Talarico and Republican nominee Ken Paxton deadlocked at 47% among likely voters. Compagno criticized Talarico's campaign for labeling the American flag a 'complicated' symbol, accusing him of being 'anti-business, anti-commerce, anti-capitalist, anti-Texas Texan.' The controversy follows Paxton's contentious primary victory after President Donald Trump endorsed him, despite Paxton's history of impeachment and criminal allegations. Talarico's campaign dismissed Compagno's remarks, stating the candidate is 'in fact a human, and not a demon in human skin.' Trump himself has expressed unease about Talarico's rise, using a pseudonym to refer to him in a Truth Social post. The race underscores growing political polari

item.title: Oxford researchers develop risk calculator to assess rare muscle side effects of statins
item.description:
Researchers at the University of Oxford have developed a new clinical calculator designed to estimate an individual’s risk of developing serious muscle disorders from statin medications. Statins are widely prescribed drugs used to lower cholesterol and reduce the risk of heart attacks and strokes, but concerns about potential muscle-related side effects often discourage patients from starting or continuing treatment. The new tool, published in The Lancet Digital Health, analyzes 22 routinely collected health factors such as age, sex, ethnicity, body mass index, smoking status, existing medical conditions, prior muscle problems, vitamin D levels, and medication use to estimate risk over one, five, and ten years.
Using data from more than 5.6 million people across England, researchers built and validated the model to ensure accuracy in predicting rare but serious muscle disorders that can require hospitalization or, in extreme cases, lead to death. Their findings suggest that over 98% o

item.title: Tom Cruise's Early Career Lessons from 'Losin’ It' (1982)
item.description: This article explores Tom Cruise's early career experiences while filming 'Losin’ It' (1982), a teen comedy directed by Curtis Hanson. At 20 years old, Cruise played Woody, a shy character who unexpectedly loses a bar fight to co-star John Stockwell. The film marked a pivotal moment in Cruise's career, as he transitioned from the highly organized set of 'Taps' to the chaotic, low-budget production of 'Losin’ It.' The production faced significant challenges, including a lack of resources, grueling six-night workweeks, and the need to cast crew members as actors. Cruise described the experience as a wake-up call, realizing that some crew members lacked passion for quality filmmaking. This realization prompted him to seek more structured projects like Francis Ford Coppola's 'The Outsiders' afterward. The article highlights how this formative experience shaped Cruise's disciplined approach to his future roles, emphasizing the importance of preparation and professionalism in the film indust

item.title: Bam Margera to Watch 'Jackass: Best & Last' Despite No Reunion Plans
item.description: Bam Margera, the former Jackass star, has stated he plans to watch the final film in the Jackass franchise, 'Jackass: Best & Last,' but confirmed a reunion with his former co-stars is 'not going to happen.' Despite his 2021 fallout with the crew, Margera expressed no ill will toward the cast, emphasizing he 'doesn’t have any bad blood' against them. However, he reiterated his desire to 'never see them ever again' due to creative decisions made by Johnny Knoxville and director Jeff Tremaine. The article details Margera's legal battle with Paramount Pictures over his dismissal from 'Jackass Forever,' where he alleged coercion and mistreatment during his rehabilitation. Now sober and focused on skateboarding as therapy, Margera credits his family for his recovery and highlights his renewed passion for the sport. The piece also notes his previous involvement in the franchise's spin-offs and his acknowledgment of archival footage in the latest film. Margera's comments reflect a complex rela

item.title: Education, political ideology, and societal divides in Spain
item.description: The article explores the complex relationship between Spain's education system and political polarization, highlighting how ideological battles over curriculum and teaching methods have deepened societal divides. It critiques the state's role in shaping educational narratives, arguing that the imposition of politically charged ideologies in schools has alienated parents and students. The text also addresses the decline in educational quality due to underfunding and the prioritization of ideological agendas over academic rigor. It references the impact of progressive policies, such as the 'woke' ideology, on shaping young minds and the resulting shift toward far-right political movements. The author calls for a return to merit-based education, emphasizing the need to separate teaching from political indoctrination. The piece underscores the broader implications of these issues, linking them to systemic failures in governance and the erosion of trust in public institutions. By analyzing

item.title: Systemic Failures in Detention Centers Lead to Suicide Attempt
item.description: The article details the tragic suicide attempt of Victor Manuel Díaz, a 36-year-old Nicaraguan man detained at El Paso's Camp East Montana facility. It highlights systemic failures in the U.S. immigration detention system, including inadequate mental health care, insufficient suicide-prevention measures, and chronic understaffing. Díaz, who had previously attempted suicide multiple times, was placed in a non-suicide-proof cell and left unattended for extended periods. The incident underscores broader issues of human rights violations, lack of accountability, and the prioritization of political narratives over detainee welfare. The article also references other reported deaths in ICE custody, calling for independent investigations and reforms to address the crisis. Key failures include the absence of proper medical evaluations, delayed medication access, and the failure to transfer detainees to higher-level care facilities. The case exemplifies the urgent need for systemic changes to pr

item.title: Farmer Gives Away 182,000 Pounds of Nectarines Amid Legal Dispute
item.description: Cesar Mora, a farmer in Fresno County, California, faced a contract dispute with Giumarra Brothers Fruit Co. that left his nectarine crop unsellable. To prevent waste, he organized a five-day giveaway at his 50-acre ranch, distributing 182,000 pounds of white-flesh nectarines to the public. The event, dubbed 'No Nectarines Wasted,' attracted crowds from across the Central Valley, turning a legal stalemate into a community celebration. Mora, who has farmed for over 30 years, emphasized his commitment to his work and the pride in sharing his harvest. The dispute over the contract, which outlined marketing and sales terms, has been ongoing for years, leaving Mora unable to sell his crop. The giveaway not only prevented spoilage but also brought unexpected recognition and gratitude from visitors, a rare occurrence for a farmer accustomed to anonymity. The event highlighted the intersection of legal challenges, agricultural practices, and community support in rural California.

item.title: Melania Trump Pushes Foster Care Legislation Through Congress Independently
item.description: First Lady Melania Trump has reportedly set a private deadline for Congress to pass her signature foster care bill, bypassing traditional White House channels. According to a Politico report, she publicly framed foster care legislation as a 'moral imperative' during a bipartisan roundtable with the House Ways and Means Committee in April. Behind closed doors, Rep. Jason Smith (R-MO) was told she wants the Fostering the Future Act on President Donald Trump's desk by the August recess. The bill, which expands housing, education, and workforce support for young people aging out of foster care, passed the House unanimously but remains stalled in the Senate. Despite White House encouragement, the administration has not actively lobbied for its passage. Melania's approach reflects her growing autonomy, as seen in her direct negotiations with Russia and Ukraine over child displacement and her public support for LGBTQIA+ communities, diverging from the administration's messaging. This marks a

item.title: Great American State Fair Closes Temporarily Due to Heatwave
item.description: A severe heatwave gripping Washington has led to the temporary closure of the Great American State Fair on the National Mall, along with delays and cancellations at other regional celebrations during the July Fourth weekend. Freedom 250, the organizer, announced on July 3, 2026, that the fair would remain closed until 5 p.m. due to dangerously high temperatures expected to exceed 100 degrees Fahrenheit. This decision follows a broader pattern of weather-related disruptions, including the postponement of outdoor events and the cancellation of planned activities. The heatwave has also impacted other festivities in the area, with organizers scrambling to adjust schedules and ensure public safety. The article highlights the challenges of hosting large-scale events during extreme weather conditions, emphasizing the need for contingency planning. While the closure of the State Fair is a significant setback for attendees, it underscores the prioritization of health and safety over entertainme

item.title: Usha Vance's Question to Trump Sparks Online Debate on His Reading Habits
item.description: Usha Vance's recent podcast episode featuring President Donald Trump has ignited widespread online discussion. During an interview on her children's literacy podcast, Vance asked Trump about his reading habits, prompting a response where he admitted to primarily reading newspapers about himself. The exchange quickly went viral, with critics and supporters interpreting it as a strategic move by Vance to highlight Trump's self-centered tendencies. Social media users speculated that Vance intentionally 'trolled' Trump with her question, while others viewed it as a genuine inquiry. The episode, recorded in the Oval Office in mid-June, also included Trump's humorous remarks about reading a picture book and joking about his weight compared to historical presidents. This incident follows previous viral moments involving Vance, such as a Father's Day episode with her husband and her advocacy for respecting Supreme Court justices. Analysts note that Vance's approach reflects a blend of politica

item.title: Herzog and de Meuron Convert 1980s Antenna Tower into Alpine Hub with Restaurant and Exhibition Space
item.description: The Titlis Tower, originally a functional 56-meter steel mast built by the Swiss postal service in the mid-1980s, has been transformed into an iconic alpine structure by Herzog and de Meuron. Located above Engelberg, the tower was never designed to be visually striking but became a landmark through its unique design. The architects inserted two cantilevered glass-and-steel volumes crosswise into the existing mast, creating a cross-shaped silhouette that contrasts with the mountain landscape. The intervention includes four vertical circulation cores, a restaurant, bar, and exhibition space spanning over 3,000 meters of elevation. Completed in 2026, this project exemplifies resource-conscious development, making the existing structure habitable and legible without competing with the natural environment. The project is part of a broader summit masterplan, highlighting the importance of respecting and enhancing existing landmarks rather than erasing them.

item.title: Study Suggests Prolonged Sitting May Impact Longevity, But Methodology Raises Questions
item.description: A recent study published in a scientific journal has sparked debate about the health risks of prolonged sitting. The research, which followed participants for a median of 12.38 years, found a correlation between extended sedentary behavior and increased mortality risk. However, the study's methodology has been criticized for relying on only 7 days of accelerometer data, raising concerns about the reliability of its conclusions. While the findings suggest that sitting for long periods may contribute to health issues, experts caution against overinterpreting the results. The study highlights the importance of regular movement, such as taking short breaks or using standing desks, to mitigate potential risks. Critics argue that the study's small sample size and limited data collection period weaken its validity. Meanwhile, public health advocates continue to emphasize the benefits of physical activity and reducing prolonged inactivity. The debate underscores the need for more rigorous rese

item.title: Valve Releases Open-Source Design for Steam Machine's E-Ink Panel
item.description: Valve has open-sourced the design for a customizable e-ink front panel for the Steam Machine, called the 'Inkterface.' The project, available on GitLab under the MIT license, provides detailed hardware specifications and assembly instructions. Key components include an Adafruit ESP32 Feather with 2MB PSRAM, an eInk Breakout Friend, a 5.83" monochrome eInk panel, and various screws and magnets. The release includes a video demonstrating the assembly process, encouraging DIY enthusiasts to build their own panels. This move aligns with Valve's trend of open-source hardware initiatives, allowing users to customize and integrate the display into their Steam Machines. The open-source nature fosters innovation and community collaboration, reflecting Valve's commitment to transparency and user-driven development in the gaming ecosystem. The project highlights the growing intersection of hardware customization and open-source technology in the gaming industry.

item.title: Confrontation Over Birthright Citizenship: Right-Wing Activist and Passerby Clash in Philadelphia
item.description: A heated confrontation erupted during a livestream in Philadelphia between right-wing influencer Jack Posobiec and a passerby, sparking a debate over birthright citizenship. Posobiec, known for promoting the debunked 'Pizzagate' hoax and advocating against birthright citizenship, faced a vocal critic during a patriotic event near Independence Hall. The man, identified as a white Anglo-Saxon Protestant, accused Posobiec of harboring white supremacist views and questioned the legality of birthright citizenship. The exchange intensified as the man referenced the Supreme Court's recent ruling upholding birthright citizenship, which overturned Trump's executive order. Posobiec defended his stance, claiming the policy is unlawful, while the man argued it was a tool for excluding non-white immigrants. The incident highlights the ongoing political polarization surrounding immigration policies and the challenges of enforcing birthright citizenship laws. The clash underscores the deepening ideol

item.title: Dreadwoods Gatekeeper: Official Gameplay Trailer for Upcoming Narrative Horror Sim
item.description: The trailer for Dreadwoods Gatekeeper, an upcoming narrative horror simulation game, showcases players taking on the role of a gatekeeper at an isolated outpost. Daily duties include inspecting trading wagons, patrolling the grounds, and maintaining the weathered station while uncovering unsettling secrets about the location. The gameplay emphasizes atmospheric tension and exploration, with a focus on the eerie environment and mysterious occurrences. Available exclusively on PC, the game invites players to immerse themselves in a creepy world filled with suspense. A Steam wishlist link is provided for interested players. The trailer features a mix of gameplay footage and cinematic sequences, highlighting the game's storytelling elements and immersive mechanics. With its blend of survival horror and simulation gameplay, Dreadwoods Gatekeeper promises a unique experience that challenges players to navigate a decaying world while unraveling its dark mysteries. The trailer also includes re

item.title: Trump Grants Pardons to Six Individuals Convicted of Emissions Tampering
item.description: President Donald Trump announced pardons for six individuals convicted of tampering with vehicle emissions controls, framing their cases as political persecution under the Biden administration. The move comes amid a broader clemency push tied to the U.S. 250th anniversary, with Trump's pardons team reportedly focusing on Clean Air Act violators, including mechanics prosecuted for disabling 'defeat devices' in diesel engines. Attorney Stewart Cables described the cases as examples of government overreach, while critics warn of potential corruption in the pardons process. The decision follows reports of a list of up to 250 clemency acts, sparking a frenzy among lawyers and lobbyists. Notable figures like Sean 'Diddy' Combs and Pras Michel sought relief but were not included in the final pardons. The White House dismissed concerns about profiting from pardons, but Democrats are investigating potential 'pay-to-play' schemes. This development underscores ongoing tensions between Trump's adm

item.title: UK Plans to Build 14 Small Modular Reactors to Supply 4.2 GW of Power by 2034
item.description: A private consortium led by European SMR developer SGE has unveiled plans to construct 14 small modular nuclear reactors (SMRs) across three UK sites, aiming to add 4.2 gigawatts (GW) of nuclear capacity by 2034. This project, submitted under the UK’s Advanced Nuclear Framework (ANF), would supply electricity to nearly eight million homes and account for 11% of the country’s electricity demand. The initiative will utilize GE Vernova Hitachi’s BWRX-300 reactor design, with partners including Samsung C&T, Laing O’Rourke, Google Cloud, and Fermi Development. The consortium emphasizes a fleet approach, with six BWRX-300 units planned for the first site, followed by two additional multi-unit sites. This strategy aims to reduce costs and construction risks through standardized modularization. The BWRX-300 is already under construction in Canada and has cleared regulatory milestones in the UK. The project seeks to strengthen the UK’s domestic nuclear supply chain and is expected to enter the

item.title: Trump's Failed State Fair and Political Struggles Highlight Public Discontent
item.description: The article critiques President Donald Trump's transformation of the Great American State Fair into a political rally, resulting in widespread cancellation of events and minimal attendance. Originally intended to celebrate the 250th anniversary of the U.S., the fair became a platform for Trump's divisive rhetoric, leading to 20 states boycotting the event. Musicians and entertainers withdrew, and only a fraction of the claimed 45,000 attendees actually showed up. The piece also highlights broader political challenges facing Trump, including a Supreme Court ruling against his birthright citizenship executive order, a federal judge blocking his mail ballot restrictions, and declining public support reflected in baby name trends. The author argues that Americans are increasingly rejecting Trump's attempts to divide the nation, emphasizing the need for unity over division. The article underscores the public's growing disillusionment with Trump's leadership and its impact on national celebr

item.title: Canada's Nuclear Energy Strategy Faces Criticism for Industry Benefits and Safety Concerns
item.description: The article critiques Canada's nuclear energy strategy, highlighting concerns that it prioritizes industry profits over public safety and environmental protection. Critics argue the policy benefits nuclear corporations while neglecting regulatory oversight, safety protocols, and long-term ecological impacts. The strategy is accused of creating a financial windfall for the nuclear sector at the expense of taxpayer accountability and environmental stewardship. Proponents, however, defend the policy as essential for energy security and reducing carbon emissions. The piece also raises questions about the potential for regulatory capture, where industry interests may influence policy decisions. Environmental groups warn of the risks associated with nuclear waste management and the potential for accidents, emphasizing the need for stricter oversight. The debate underscores tensions between economic development and public safety in energy policy. Overall, the article calls for a balanced appr

item.title: Drones Used to Enforce Firework Regulations During Independence Day Celebrations
item.description: The article discusses the increasing use of drones by law enforcement agencies in California to monitor and enforce firework regulations during Independence Day celebrations. As traditional methods of monitoring illegal fireworks prove insufficient, police departments are turning to drone technology to track and apprehend individuals violating local ordinances. The piece highlights the growing trend of cities like Riverside, San Bernardino, and Santa Ana deploying drones equipped with advanced cameras and sensors to identify and locate unauthorized fireworks displays. This shift reflects broader efforts to enhance public safety and reduce the risks associated with illegal pyrotechnics, such as property damage and potential injuries. The article also notes the challenges of balancing enforcement with privacy concerns, as drones can inadvertently capture footage of private property. Additionally, it mentions the role of certified fireworks displays in providing legal, safe alternatives f

item.title: Andy Warhol's Uncle Sam: A Photographic Portrait
item.description: The article discusses Andy Warhol's 1981 photographic series depicting Uncle Sam, commissioned by gallerist Ronald Feldman. James A. Mahoney, an actor known for advertising work, was hired to portray the iconic American figure. Warhol captured over 70 Polaroids of Mahoney at 860 Broadway, using a combination of line screens and halftone techniques to create the final artwork. Makeup artist Jac Colello styled Mahoney's costume, including a star-spangled top hat, white beard, and striped pants. The series, part of Warhol's 'Myths' project, highlights his innovative approach to blending drawing and photography. Mahoney expressed gratitude for the silkscreen print in a 1981 letter to Warhol and Feldman, underscoring the collaborative nature of the project. The piece offers insight into Warhol's artistic process and the cultural significance of Uncle Sam as a symbol of Americana.

item.title: Extreme Heat Forces Closure of Trump's State Fair
item.description: The Great American State Fair on the National Mall was forced to close early due to a record-breaking heatwave that gripped Washington, D.C., with temperatures nearing 100°F and heat indexes exceeding 113°F. Organizers suspended the event, planning to resume at 5 p.m., as the city activated an extreme heat alert through July 5. At least 20 locations broke daily temperature records, with cities like New York and Newark experiencing hotter conditions than Phoenix. The heatwave disrupted multiple events, including Philadelphia's Independence Day parade and parades in Norristown, Pennsylvania, and Haddon Township, New Jersey. A study by World Weather Attribution linked the heat intensity to fossil fuel pollution, emphasizing the urgency of climate action. The extreme weather also strained the power grid, prompting Energy Secretary Chris Wright to redirect data center power to residential air conditioning. Officials urged attendees to take breaks in the shade, stay hydrated, and monitor for

item.title: Democrats Prepare Investigations Into Trump's Financial Disclosures and Inner Circle
item.description: The article discusses Democrats preparing a hostile audit of President Donald Trump and his inner circle, aiming to investigate his financial disclosures and potential conflicts of interest. Trump's 2025 financial report, a 927-page filing, reveals he earned over $2.2 billion in 2025, with crypto ventures contributing over $1 billion. His largest income source was $635 million in royalties from the $TRUMP meme coin. Democrats argue that Trump's financial setup may violate the Emoluments Clause, citing his legal settlements with major media outlets. House Speaker Mike Johnson warned donors about potential targeting under a Democratic House majority, while Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez criticized his comments as a 'protection racket.' The White House denies conflicts of interest, asserting Trump acts in the public's best interest. The piece highlights the political tensions and scrutiny surrounding Trump's finances and the potential for extensive investigations if Democrats regain contro

item.title: The Onion's Satirical Take on Infowars and Its Legal Battle with Alex Jones
item.description: The Onion, a satirical news organization, has launched a parody of Alex Jones' Infowars platform, aiming to highlight the legal and ethical controversies surrounding the conspiracy theorist. The parody, titled 'Infowars: The Show,' is part of The Onion's ongoing efforts to satirize media manipulation and misinformation. The original Infowars platform, known for promoting conspiracy theories and pseudoscientific claims, has faced legal challenges, including a lawsuit from the families of Sandy Hook victims. The Onion's parody is designed to critique the spread of misinformation and the legal battles over content ownership. The article details the legal dispute between Infowars and the Sandy Hook families, which has led to the platform's financial decline. The parody's creators argue that by keeping Infowars' value high through satire, they can prevent its liquidation to cover Alex Jones' debts. The piece also explores the broader implications of media ownership, the role of satire in pu

item.title: New macOS Malware Steals Credentials Using Advanced Techniques
item.description: Researchers discovered a previously unseen macOS malware called PamStealer, which employs sophisticated methods to steal user credentials. The malware operates in two stages: the first is a disk image disguised as a clipboard manager named Maccy, which is compiled as AppleScript. The second stage uses Rust-based code to exploit macOS's Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) interface to validate login passwords before transmitting them to an attacker's server. The malware mimics a system authorization request, tricking users into entering their passwords. Once validated, it displays a decoy message claiming the file is damaged to avoid suspicion. Additional tactics include requesting full disk access and accessing Ethereum accounts. The malware's use of local PAM validation, rather than external processes, makes it harder to detect. Security experts note that this highlights vulnerabilities in macOS's password prompts, which have been exploited by malware for years. The incident unders

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's Wedding Celebration in New York City
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are set to celebrate their wedding in a high-profile event in New York City, drawing significant attention from fans and media. The article details the scale of the celebration, including security measures, fan reactions, and the couple's public statements about their relationship. Security personnel were observed securing the Madison Square Garden venue, while fans in cities like Vienna and New York gathered to commemorate the event. The piece highlights the couple's journey, with Travis Kelce emphasizing the authenticity of their relationship despite their celebrity status. Taylor Swift, known for her role as a bridesmaid in past events, is now the central figure in her own wedding. The article also touches on the broader cultural impact of their union, with fans reflecting on the supportive nature of their partnership. Quotes from both individuals and fans underscore the significance of the event, blending personal milestones with public spectacle. The

item.title: Massive surveillance expansion under Trump administration faces growing opposition
item.description: The article details the expanding use of Flock Safety's surveillance cameras by U.S. law enforcement, particularly under the Trump administration, for purposes far beyond traditional law enforcement. These AI-powered license plate readers are being deployed for immigration enforcement, tracking pregnant individuals across states, and monitoring constitutionally protected protests. Over 80,000 devices are installed nationwide, generating 20 billion scans monthly, with minimal oversight. Cities like Santa Cruz and Wisconsin counties have terminated contracts due to privacy concerns and potential civil liberties violations. The ACLU's 'Get the Flock Out' campaign highlights the lack of transparency and regulation in data collection. Bipartisan efforts in Congress aim to restrict ALPR use for non-toll collection purposes, while cybersecurity breaches have exposed customer data. The controversy reflects broader concerns about government overreach and the ethical implications of mass surveil

item.title: Energy Storage Solutions for Seasonal Demand
item.description: The article discusses the limitations of current battery storage systems for seasonal energy needs, proposing hydrogen-based storage solutions as a viable alternative. It highlights the EU's focus on methane storage despite lower efficiency compared to pumped hydro. The text analyzes renewable energy patterns, noting China's potential for nuclear development due to its challenging climate conditions. It also critiques the lack of international electrical grid infrastructure, suggesting a need for a global network similar to fiber optics. Comments address the geographical and meteorological challenges of renewable energy deployment, emphasizing the importance of strategic resource locations. The piece underscores the necessity of balancing energy production with storage solutions to address both daily and seasonal demand fluctuations.

item.title: Corporate Tax Cuts and Campaign Donations: GOP Lawmakers Face Scrutiny
item.description: A report by Unrig Our Economy reveals a pay-to-play dynamic where major corporations benefiting from Trump-era tax cuts are funneling donations to GOP lawmakers. The analysis highlights seven House Republicans who supported the 2025 budget law, which extended corporate tax breaks while slashing Medicaid and federal nutrition programs. Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) received over $57,000 in donations from PACs representing 3M, Amazon, Walmart, and AT&T—companies that collectively gained billions in tax advantages. Amazon’s effective federal tax rate dropped to 1.37% under the law, despite soaring profits. The report also details Rep. Rob Bresnahan’s (PA) ties to FirstEnergy, which secured $500 million in depreciation deductions. Bresnahan, who owns stock in all contributing companies, faced scrutiny for selling assets in Medicaid providers and hospitals before voting to cut funding. The report condemns the GOP for prioritizing corporate interests over working families, urging la

item.title: Pulitzer Prize-Winning Author Criticizes Trump's Immigration Policies and Historical Cruelty
item.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning author Viet Thanh Nguyen published a scathing essay criticizing U.S. President Donald Trump's administration for transforming immigration enforcement into a spectacle of cruelty. The essay, released to mark the nation's 250th anniversary, argues that America's defining trait is not liberty or opportunity but systemic cruelty. Nguyen links Trump's policies to historical patterns of displacement, slavery, and exploitation of immigrant labor, framing them as part of an imperial legacy. He condemns bipartisan U.S. support for Israel's actions in Gaza and the war on Iran as examples of imperial overreach. The author calls for humility and repentance to save the country, warning that current leadership, described as 'led by its worst,' cannot fix systemic issues without rejecting American exceptionalism. Nguyen's critique extends to the U.S.'s founding violence, suggesting that internal contradictions and external pressures will drive necessary change.

item.title: Photographer Captures Melting Ice Cream in Long Exposure Art
item.description: Michael Massaia's 'Meltsicles' series explores the nostalgic beauty of melting ice cream through long exposure photography. The artist captures the transformation of frozen treats into drippy, ephemeral forms, evoking childhood memories of sticky hands and sweet, soupy puddles. Using extended exposure times, Massaia transforms the gradual melting process into striking visual metaphors, highlighting the interplay of light, texture, and time. His work reflects on the fleeting nature of moments, turning the simple act of ice cream melting into a meditative, almost zen-like state. The series challenges viewers to appreciate the impermanence of joy and the artistry in everyday phenomena. Massaia's photographs, which blend technical precision with poetic sensibility, invite contemplation on the intersection of art and natural processes. The project underscores how ordinary experiences can be reimagined as profound, transient works of art. Through his lens, the melting ice cream becomes a sym

item.title: Ex-Trump Official Warns of Democratic Crisis Under Trump's Leadership
item.description: Alexander Willis, a former Trump administration official and senior editor at Raw Story, warns that the United States faces a critical decision about its future under President Donald Trump. In an op-ed published on the eve of the nation's 250th anniversary, Willis argues that the foundational pillars of American democracy—rule of law, free dissent, separation of powers, and public official loyalty—are under threat. He draws parallels to the 2001 September 11 attacks, highlighting the passengers of United Flight 93 who chose to fight against hijackers rather than comply. Willis contends that this act demonstrated the power of popular sovereignty, where authority originates from the people. He criticizes Trump's actions, such as jailing critics and deploying domestic troops, as consequences of voter abdication. Willis urges Americans to recognize their agency in rebuilding democracy, emphasizing that while crises may arise, the nation retains the capacity to recover through collective a

item.title: Digital account created for children's safety
item.description: The article discusses a media report highlighting an individual's creation of a digital account intended for their children's use. While the original title employs clickbait tactics by emphasizing sensational language ('According to a media report' and 'can be used for their children'), the content focuses on practical digital account management for family purposes. The summary clarifies that the report centers on a parent's initiative to establish a secure digital space for children, potentially addressing online safety or educational resources. Key points include the individual's motivation, the account's intended applications, and implications for digital literacy. The article also touches on broader societal trends regarding parental involvement in managing children's digital footprints. However, the original title's emphasis on media reports and the phrasing 'can be used for their children' may exaggerate the significance of the account's purpose, making it more attention-grabbing

item.title: U.S. Space Force Collaborates with Private Startups for Orbital Reconnaissance Missions
item.description: Two U.S. space startups, True Anomaly and Rocket Lab, completed a high-stakes orbital mission for the U.S. Space Force, demonstrating advanced reconnaissance capabilities in space. The exercise, named Victus Haze, involved a rendezvous between Rocket Lab's Puma spacecraft and True Anomaly's Jackal satellite, with the latter capturing detailed imagery of the former. This mission, conducted just 16 hours after Puma's launch, showcased the ability to track and inspect satellites in orbit—a critical capability amid growing concerns about space weaponization by nations like China and Russia. True Anomaly's CEO, Even Rogers, emphasized the need for enhanced surveillance to counter emerging threats. The operation marked a significant milestone in private-sector involvement in military space operations, with plans for future exercises involving evasive maneuvers and complex inspections. The collaboration highlights the U.S. Space Force's reliance on commercial partners to address gaps in space

item.title: Trump calls for removal of Fed Governor Lisa Cook amid economic tensions
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump has publicly stated his intention to pursue the removal of Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook, a move that has sparked debate among economists and political analysts. The comment comes amid ongoing discussions about monetary policy and its impact on the economy. Trump's remarks, made during a live coverage event, suggest a potential shift in the administration's approach to central banking. Critics argue that such statements could undermine the independence of the Federal Reserve, while supporters claim it reflects a necessary check on regulatory overreach. The Fed's role in setting interest rates and managing inflation remains a contentious issue, with Trump's administration advocating for policies that prioritize economic growth over inflation control. Analysts note that Trump's comments could influence future policy decisions, particularly if his political allies gain power in Congress. The potential removal of Cook, who has been a vocal advocate f

item.title: Trump's Obsession with Washington D.C. Renovations Amid Legal Challenges
item.description: President Donald Trump's fixation with transforming Washington, D.C., has intensified since his return to the White House in January 2025, according to a former campaign official. The obsession began during a motorcade ride to face election interference charges in August 2023, when Trump reportedly noticed the city's deteriorating state, including homeless encampments and neglected infrastructure. Since then, Trump has launched an extensive renovation project costing over $1 billion, blending private and public funds. Key initiatives include a new East Wing ballroom, a 250-foot Triumphal Arch near Arlington National Cemetery, a Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repainted 'American flag blue' (which later developed algae issues), and a golf course overhaul. Administration officials describe Trump as fixated on these projects, often discussing them during unrelated meetings and keeping mock-ups on his desk. Critics, including Rep. Ro Khanna, warn about potential taxpayer exposure and lack

item.title: FDA Approves ZYN Pouches as Less Harmful Alternative to Cigarettes, But Advises Against Use
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has authorized 20 ZYN nicotine pouch products to carry a 'modified risk' claim, stating that switching from cigarettes to these pouches reduces risks of mouth cancer, heart disease, lung cancer, stroke, emphysema, and chronic bronchitis. However, the FDA explicitly warns that nicotine pouches are not safe or approved as smoking cessation tools. The decision allows Swedish Match, the manufacturer of ZYN, to market specific products as a less harmful option for adults who fully transition away from cigarettes. This ruling comes amid rapid global growth of nicotine pouches, with 23.4 billion units sold in 2024, a 50% increase from the previous year. The World Health Organization (WHO) cautions that nicotine pouches are gaining popularity among adolescents and young adults due to their 'strong youth appeal and high addiction potential,' raising concerns about attracting new users. While the FDA's modified risk tobacco product (MRTP) order applies

item.title: Tech Billionaire Accuses Pope of Supporting Chinese Communist Interests in AI Regulation
item.description: Right-wing tech billionaire Peter Thiel has accused Pope Leo XIV of acting as a 'Chinese communist agent' for criticizing artificial intelligence (AI) in a 42,000-word encyclical. Thiel, a co-founder of PayPal and Palantir, claimed the pope's call for strict AI regulation could jeopardize the U.S.'s global competitiveness in advanced technology development. The Pope warned that unregulated AI could heighten the 'risk of dehumanization' worldwide. Thiel's remarks, made at the Aspen Ideas Festival, echo his long-standing disdain for AI critics, whom he has previously labeled as 'agents for the Antichrist.' Journalist Christopher Hale noted Thiel's history of linking the Pope to apocalyptic symbolism, including suggesting the pontiff might be a 'manifestation of the Antichrist.' Thiel also warned of a 'democratic-socialist takeover' of the Democratic Party, citing recent victories by Democratic Socialists of America candidates. Additionally, Thiel is reportedly considering establishing a

item.title: Dr. Elliot McGucken's Spacetime Light Sculptures
item.description: Dr. Elliot McGucken, a fine art photographer and physicist, merges scientific theory with artistic vision through his 'Spacetime Light Cone Sculptures dx4/dt=ic' series. Inspired by Einstein's relativity, he uses drone-mounted lights and long exposure to create glowing light cones and hourglass shapes in night skies over remote American landscapes. These ephemeral artworks, visible above locations like the Trona Pinnacles and Alabama Hills, visualize the relationship between space, time, and light. The project balances complex scientific concepts with visual wonder, with the vast, silent landscapes amplifying the precision and strangeness of the light forms. McGucken's work draws parallels to the innovative light installations of Reuben Wu, offering an otherworldly perspective on cosmic phenomena. The series exemplifies how technology and art can collaboratively explore abstract scientific ideas, transforming them into tangible, breathtaking experiences. The photographs capture the int

item.title: Keeping Pets Safe During the Fourth of July Fireworks
item.description: The Fourth of July celebrations often pose risks to pets, with a significant increase in lost animals being brought to shelters on July 5th. Elizabeth Oreck, senior manager of community programs for Best Friends Animal Society, highlights that this day sees the highest intake of lost pets in Los Angeles. To prevent pets from escaping, owners are advised to secure their homes, create a safe space with soft music, and ensure pets are indoors before fireworks begin. Microchipping is emphasized as critical for reuniting lost pets with their owners, with details on costs and availability in Los Angeles County. If pets do escape, community alerts and social media outreach are recommended, along with utilizing free services like PetCo Lost Love. Animal Services has also called for foster volunteers to manage the influx of animals. The article underscores the importance of preparedness and community involvement to ensure pet safety during the holiday.

item.title: Stage Collapse at Trump-Linked State Fair Raises Safety Concerns
item.description: A stage collapse at the Great American State Fair, organized by the Trump-linked group Freedom 250, has sparked concerns about safety protocols and potential legal consequences for the president. The incident occurred during the fair's 250th-anniversary celebration in Washington, D.C., where performers narrowly avoided injury when part of the stage gave way, sending debris crashing down. Journalist Ryan Grim warned that such negligence could lead to genuine prosecution if someone dies, emphasizing the danger of substandard construction. The fair, which faced initial backlash for its Trump connections, has also experienced power outages, food spoilage, and a stalled Ferris wheel. Organizers have not yet commented on the incident, which was first reported by independent journalist Aaron Parnas. Grim's social media post highlighted the risks of cutting corners on safety, noting that equipment falling from such heights is deadly. The event's challenges, including empty booths and criticism

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce to Wed in New York City
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are set to tie the knot in a high-profile wedding event in New York City, marking a significant moment for the celebrity couple. The wedding celebration, scheduled for Friday at Madison Square Garden, follows a smaller gathering the day before. This event has sparked widespread speculation and media attention, with details about the guest list, timing, and venue becoming focal points of public interest. The couple, known for their public relationship, has maintained a level of privacy regarding the specifics of their nuptials, leading to increased curiosity among fans and the media. The wedding's location at Madison Square Garden, a iconic venue in New York, adds to the event's grandeur and cultural significance. The ceremony is expected to be a blend of personal celebration and public spectacle, reflecting the couple's status in the entertainment industry. As the wedding approaches, details about the event's logistics, including the guest list and timing,

item.title: GTA VI Launches Digital-Only with Physical Editions Containing Download Codes Only
item.description: Grand Theft Auto VI, one of the most anticipated video games in years, has sparked significant backlash not primarily over its high price tag of £69.99 in the UK and $79.99 in the US, but because its physical editions will not include a traditional game disc. Instead, buyers will receive a box containing only a download code for the digital version. This decision by Rockstar Games highlights the ongoing shift in the gaming industry from physical media to fully digital distribution. Critics argue this erodes consumer ownership, as digital licenses can be revoked or become inaccessible due to server issues or account problems in the future. Supporters point out benefits like easier pre-loading, reduced manufacturing costs, and lower environmental impact from physical production and shipping. The move follows a broader industry trend where major titles increasingly favor digital delivery. Gamers have expressed concerns about preserving physical collections for archival purposes and the ab

item.title: Global Nuclear Disarmament Advocacy and Radiation-Free Environment Campaign
item.description: The article critiques the global push for nuclear disarmament and environmental safety, highlighting the 22,000-mile journey of activists advocating for a radiation-free world. It discusses the challenges of dismantling nuclear arsenals, the health risks of radiation exposure, and the need for international cooperation. The text emphasizes the urgency of transitioning to renewable energy sources to mitigate environmental degradation.
